notes: Jenkins converted second-half role and minutes into a clean over.
Draymond stalled at 5 rebounds despite competitive script and 22 minutes.
Warriors rebound tree widened late with Santos, Podziemski, Spencer and Yurtseven all absorbing boards.
Naz Reid absence did not flow to Randle rebound share as expected.
Gobert and Dosunmu captured more of Minnesota's glass than projected.
Portland offensive rebound pressure and Robert Williams bench surge distorted the ownership map.
Poeltl remained the cleanest anchor read and cleared comfortably.
Jokic never approached pregame over line and validated the halftime under pivot.
Barnes and Braun both stalled below liveflow over thresholds as rebound ownership flattened across both teams.

key_takeaways: Poeltl and Avdija validated as strong pregame ownership targets.
Randle miss exposed a rebound share projection gap after Naz Reid absence.
Jokic over was a bad pregame ceiling chase but Jokic halftime under was the correct adaptation.
3-leg boosted LiveFlow structure underperformed versus 2-leg discipline thesis.
system_actions: Downgrade non-anchor secondary rebound overs when ownership map is not dominant.
Upgrade adaptive ownership learning around replacement-minute environments.
Preserve halftime under leverage when anchor rebounding share is visibly suppressed.
